execution.summary: Added a prominent note in the StatsD README 'Application Section Options' section clarifying that the [app] section is a namespace/container that does not create dashboard charts by itself, and that users must add chart definition sections to see synthetic charts.
acceptance_checks: The edit explicitly states that [app] is a namespace/container that does not produce dashboard charts on its own.
The edit directs users to add [chart_id] sections for synthetic charts to appear on the dashboard.
The edit explains that without chart definitions, only private charts (if enabled) will be visible.
Existing option descriptions (name, metrics, private charts, gaps, memory mode, history) remain unchanged.
No Learn links are added; only an in-document anchor link to #chart-definitions is used.
The note appears in the 'Application Section Options' section before the option list.
execution.commit_title: docs(statsd): clarify [app] section is a namespace requiring chart definitions
execution.pr_title: docs(statsd): clarify [app] section is a namespace requiring chart definitions
execution.pr_body:
## Summary
Clarifies in the StatsD plugin README that the `[app]` configuration section is a namespace/container for grouping metrics and setting defaults — it does **not** create dashboard charts by itself. Users must add `[chart_id]` chart definition sections to see synthetic charts on the dashboard.

## User Request
"My problem is the settings I've defined in the [app] section do not show up in the dashboard."

## Reason for Change
Users configuring StatsD synthetic charts often define only an `[app]` section and expect dashboard charts to appear. The `[app]` section is a namespace that groups metrics and sets defaults; synthetic charts require separate `[chart_id]` sections with `dimension` lines. This note proactively explains the relationship, preventing the most common configuration mistake.

## Audit Context
- **Topic**: statsd_app_section_settings_not_appearing_in_dashboard
- **Decision verdict**: surgical_edit
- **Validation**: The [app] section creates a STATSD_APP struct (statsd.c:1304-1321) with chart=NULL; synthetic charts only render when dimensions_linked_count > 0 (statsd.c:2306).
